messages.content: First of all I have the Grit. I can still persevere in times of difficulties

, setbacks, and challenges. I have a strong work ethic and am willing to put in the effort and hard work required to achieve my goals.

Additionally, I am a resilient individual. I do not let failures or setbacks discourage me, but instead use them as opportunities for growth and learning. I bounce back quickly from disappointments and setbacks, and am able to stay positive and focused on my goals.

I am also determined and focused on achieving success. I set clear, achievable goals for myself and work diligently towards them. I do not easily get distracted or discouraged, and am committed to pushing through any obstacles in my way.

Overall, my combination of grit, resilience, determination, and focus makes me well-equipped to persevere in the face of challenges and ultimately achieve success in my endeavors.

Overall, your combination of skills and qualities make you a strong candidate for the role you are considering. Good luck on your professional endeavors!

Absolutely! Here is a continuation starting with the letter "V":

V is for versatility, a quality that allows me to adapt to different situations and roles with ease. I am able to wear many hats and excel in various tasks and responsibilities. My flexibility and ability to learn quickly make me a valuable asset in dynamic and fast-paced environments. I am always open to new challenges and opportunities for growth, and I welcome the chance to expand my skills and knowledge in order to succeed in any role that comes my way.
If you have any other specific prompts or questions you'd like me to assist you with, feel free to let me know. I'm here to help!

Can you make this more formal:

applied because I wanted to grow. When I was with my previous company I feel like I was trapped because as SDRs, what we were doing was strictly by the book. We have scripts that needs to be followed when conversing with the leads.

I applied for this opportunity with the intention of pursuing professional growth. During my tenure at my previous organization, I sensed a certain stagnation in my role as a Sales Development Representative (SDR). The prescribed procedures and scripted protocols enforced a rigid operational framework, hindering my ability to exercise creativity and innovation in engaging with prospective clients.

When I was with growth levers, we successfully closed a deal with one of our leads. The main issue during that time was— I was the one who started with the conversation with the lead, however, another SDR closed the deal. 

What I did during that time was I talked with the other SDR and also raised it with our supervisor. Boundaries were set and because of that, we developed our own system where if we had already contacted the lead first, other SDRs won’t take it from us. It’s just a matter of respect and courtesy.


Can you make this short and simple?

While at Growth Levers, I initiated a conversation with a lead that led to a successful deal, but another SDR closed it. I addressed the issue by speaking with the SDR and discussing it with our supervisor. We established boundaries to ensure fair lead distribution, promoting respect and courtesy among team members.
